witnesses.verdict: ASP is essentially the same audit normal form under a colliding name

witnesses.future_capability_changed: future operations available under witness/right/path/certification constraints
witnesses.verdict: best internal label but still absorbed by established feasible-action formalisms

witnesses.state_held_fixed: same endpoints, map behavior, and naive lost-label set

witnesses.future_capability_changed: whether the claim/evidence may be used later
witnesses.candidate_cause: admissibility tokens, guard conditions, pre-registration, typed forgotten structure
witnesses.verdict: admissibility is a policy layer over future operations, not FCP itself

witnesses.future_capability_changed: maintained usability, repair, challenge, emergence-platform operation
witnesses.candidate_cause: maintenance budget applied to witnesses, rights, repair paths, and record support
witnesses.strongest_existing_absorber: viability kernels/control/resource accounting with enriched state
witnesses.verdict: useful audit pattern; not independent of viability/control/resource frameworks

prior_art.reason: If the state includes witnesses, rights, access, provenance, and certificates, FCP is the reachable/action set.
Absorbs FCP when the viability condition is survival of the declared capability set over a horizon.
Directly models feasible future opportunities under constraints and rights.
Close conceptual neighbor for real opportunities, but less formal about witnesses and certifications.
Rights, admissible moves, incentives, challenge rules, and authority are native mechanism variables.
Captures agent-environment action possibilities but may omit formal proof/admissibility structure.
Absorbs FCP if policies range over witness/right-bearing states and expected future actions.
Captures executable actions but usually lacks provenance, certification, and challenge rights.
Directly captures lineage, custody, attribution, replay, and certification prerequisites.
Directly captures authorized operations over protected resources and evidence objects.
Capability tokens, leases, certificates, and quorum/challenge windows absorb many packet and consensus cases.

strongest_common_object: A task-indexed constrained capability structure over a visible state: operations plus requirements for witnesses, rights, reconstruction paths, provenance, maintenance/access, certifications, and an admissibility policy. It is an enriched state/action object, not a new physical primitive.
weakest_point: Once the hidden structure is admitted into the state, the apparent same-state split disappears into standard enriched reachability, opportunity-set, provenance, access-control, mechanism-design, or viability machinery. Novelty survives only against coarse state descriptions, which is not enough.
closest_prior_art: Enriched reachability and opportunity-set economics are closest at the abstract level. Provenance systems, access-control/capability systems, and mechanism design are closest for the evidence/rights cases. Viability kernels absorb the maintenance/horizon reading.
strongest_separation_witness: T123 is the cleanest same-state witness: same raw payload, same immediate measurement result, and same coarse detector summary, but different packet wrappers remove certification, reconstruction, challenge, lineage, and claim-review operations.
strongest_absorption_witness: The Git/history witness is the strongest absorption case: the endpoint tree is the same only under a deliberately coarse state. Version-control theory already treats branch history, merge base, and signed tags as operational state.
recommendation_rationale: Formalize narrowly as an audit normal form named only provisionally as FCP. Do not promote. The structure explains the witness family, but it is reducible to existing enriched state/action frameworks.
claim_impact_note: No core claim upgrade. FCP should be treated as a cross-domain diagnostic for missing witness/right/provenance/reconstruction state, not as a new ontology.
